mechanism: Broad tyrosine kinase inhibition including FLT3 (and BCR‑ABL)
additional_notes: Activity reported in some FLT3‑ITD AML lines; multi‑target profile.

mechanism: Inhibits Src, Abl and other kinases relevant to AML survival pathways
additional_notes: Reported activity in some AML lines; potency often low‑tens nM to sub‑µM.

mechanism: Inhibits thioredoxin reductase causing oxidative stress and cell death
additional_notes: Reported AML activity; typically sub‑ to low‑µM.

mechanism: Mitochondrial uncoupling and modulation of WNT/STAT3 and other pathways
additional_notes: Reported anti‑leukemia activity; often sub‑ to low‑µM in cancer models.

mechanism: Proteasome and aldehyde dehydrogenase modulation; copper‑dependent cytotoxicity
additional_notes: Activity frequently requires copper (Cu(II)) for maximal effect; reported low‑µM potency.

mechanism: Disrupts microtubules and affects multiple cancer pathways
additional_notes: Reported antileukemia activity; typically low‑µM range.

mechanism: Inhibits BTK (and some off‑target kinases) affecting survival signaling in subsets of AML
additional_notes: Some AML lines show sensitivity; potency is line‑dependent and generally low‑µM.

mechanism: Inhibits CDK4/6 causing G1 cell‑cycle arrest
additional_notes: Reported activity in some AML contexts; typically sub‑ to low‑µM.
Very potent (low nM) in MAPK‑addicted contexts; higher IC50s if MAPK not driving.

mechanism: Inhibits the 26S proteasome, disrupting proteostasis and inducing apoptosis
additional_notes: Reported activity in AML cell lines; potency ranges low nM to sub‑µM.

mechanism: Inhibits HSP90, leading to destabilization of multiple oncoproteins
additional_notes: Also known as Tanespimycin (17‑AAG); reported low nM to sub‑µM potency in AML.

mechanism: Inhibits NAMPT, depleting cellular NAD and causing cytotoxicity
additional_notes: Also called APO866; potent cytotoxicity reported in AML and leukemic stem cells.
Activity in AML subsets; potency context dependent (low nM to sub‑µM).

mechanism: Raises lysosomal pH and blocks autophagy/lysosomal degradation
additional_notes: Also referenced: hydroxychloroquine; mid‑ to high‑µM needed for single‑agent effects in vitro.

mechanism: Inhibits mitochondrial complex I and induces metabolic stress
additional_notes: Reported anti‑leukemic effects at millimolar concentrations in vitro.

mechanism: Inhibits HMG‑CoA reductase, affecting cholesterol synthesis, prenylation, and apoptosis pathways
additional_notes: Reported anti‑AML activity in vitro (low to mid‑µM).
